prompt: Write a Python module with a rate limiter class (token bucket), a decorator that applies it to any function, and a small demo showing it throttling.
Create an HTML page with a working pomodoro timer: start/pause/reset buttons, a session counter, and a settings panel for work/break durations. Inline CSS+JS.

Three friends split a restaurant bill of $127.50 with a 18% tip. Alice pays twice what Bob pays, Carol pays $10 more than Bob. Work out each person's share, verify the total, and explain the algebra step by step.
Compare SQLite, DuckDB, and PostgreSQL for a single-user desktop analytics app: storage model, concurrency, SQL coverage, and packaging. End with a recommendation.
Write a CLI todo app in Python: add/list/done/delete commands, JSON file persistence, and unit tests for the persistence layer.
Plan a 3-day Tokyo itinerary for a first-time visitor who likes food and technology: day-by-day schedule, one neighborhood per day, transit notes, and a budget estimate.

A warehouse has 3 packing stations with different rates (40, 55, 70 boxes/hr). Orders arrive at 150 boxes/hr for 8 hours. Determine whether the stations keep up, where the backlog peaks, and the minimum rate upgrade needed at the slowest station.
Summarize the tradeoffs between REST, GraphQL, and gRPC for a mobile app backend, then propose which to use for a chat application and justify it.
Build a Python script that watches a folder, computes SHA-256 of new files, appends results to a CSV log, and prints a colored summary. Include a test with a temp directory.
A subscription service has 10,000 users, 5% monthly churn, and adds 600 new users/month. Model the user count monthly for a year, find the steady state, and explain whether growth is sustainable.

Evaluate three approaches to full-text search for a 1M-document knowledge base (PostgreSQL FTS, Elasticsearch, embedded vector search). Compare setup cost, query capability, and operational burden; recommend one.
Draft a one-page onboarding guide for a new junior developer joining a small Python team: first-week checklist, key tools, code review etiquette, and who to ask what.
